events.title: Jennifer Lopez turns 57
events.blurb: Global superstar Jennifer Lopez celebrates her 57th birthday today.

angles.name: J.Lo's Birthday Playlist
angles.copy: It's J.Lo's birthday! We're celebrating 57 years of hits from the Bronx to the world. What's your ultimate J.Lo track?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The legendary frontman of The Rolling Stones, Mick Jagger, celebrates his 83rd birthday today.

angles.name: Jagger's Enduring Energy
angles.copy: 83 years young and still moving like Jagger! Happy Birthday to the iconic Rolling Stones frontman. What's your favourite Mick Jagger moment?

events.title: MTV launched in 1981
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched on this day in 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' being the first music video aired.

angles.name: MTV's Legacy
angles.copy: Remember when MTV actually played music videos? On this day in '81, a revolution began with 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. What was the first music video that truly blew your mind?

events.title: Oasis released 'Definitely Maybe' in 1994
events.blurb: Oasis' groundbreaking debut album 'Definitely Maybe' was released on August 29, 1994, propelling the Manchester band to stardom.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Anniversary
angles.copy: 32 years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and changed the game. From 'Supersonic' to 'Live Forever', which track still gives you goosebumps?

events.title: Woodstock Festival began in 1969
events.blurb: The iconic Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in music history, kicked off on August 15, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Woodstock Remembered
angles.copy: 57 years ago today, half a million gathered for three days of peace and music at Woodstock. What's your favourite legendary performance from the festival?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Today marks the anniversary of the passing of the 'King of Rock and Roll', Elvis Presley, who would have been 91.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: On this day, we remember the unparalleled talent of Elvis Presley, who would have been 91. His legacy as the King of Rock and Roll lives on.

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's death
events.blurb: Influential rapper Tupac Shakur, born June 16, 1971, was fatally shot on September 7, 1996, and passed away on September 13, 1996, at the age of 25.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering Tupac Shakur, a voice that defined a generation, who would have been 55 this year. His impact on music and culture remains profound.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death
events.blurb: Guitar icon Jimi Hendrix, born November 27, 1942, tragically passed away on September 18, 1970, at the age of 27.

angles.name: Jimi Hendrix Tribute
angles.copy: On this day, we honour the genius of Jimi Hendrix, a true guitar revolutionary who would have been 84. His music still electrifies.

events.title: The Beatles released 'Abbey Road' in 1969
events.blurb: The Beatles' eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', featuring iconic tracks like 'Come Together' and 'Here Comes the Sun', was released on September 26, 1969.

angles.name: Abbey Road Anniversary
angles.copy: That iconic zebra crossing! The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' album, released 57 years ago, is a timeless masterpiece. What's your favourite track from the album?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, the formidable lead singer of The Pretenders, celebrates her 75th birthday today.

angles.name: Chrissie Hynde's Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 75th Birthday to the legendary Chrissie Hynde! Still rocking and inspiring. What's your go-to Pretenders track?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: The unmistakable voice of Oasis and solo star, Liam Gallagher, celebrates his 54th birthday today.

angles.name: Our Kid's Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 54th Birthday to Liam Gallagher! Still one of the most iconic frontmen in British music. What's your favourite Liam moment or song?

events.title: John Lennon released 'Imagine' in 1971
events.blurb: John Lennon's powerful anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the US on October 11, 1971, from his album of the same name.

angles.name: Imagine's Message
angles.copy: 55 years since John Lennon asked us to 'Imagine'. A timeless message of peace that still resonates today. What does this song mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod in 2001
events.blurb: The revolutionary first-generation Apple iPod, which transformed how we listen to music, was unveiled on October 23, 2001.

angles.name: iPod Revolution
angles.copy: 25 years ago, Apple dropped the first iPod, putting '1,000 songs in your pocket'. Did you get one of the originals, and what was on your first playlist?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated as James Bond Day, marking the 1962 release of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No'.

angles.name: 007 Day
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! From the gadgets to the theme tunes, what's your favourite thing about 007? Licence to thrill all day long!

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Today we remember John Lennon, who would have celebrated his 86th birthday.

angles.name: Remembering John Lennon
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we celebrate the enduring legacy of John Lennon. His music and message continue to inspire.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: The legendary British comedian and actor, John Cleese, known for Monty Python and Fawlty Towers, celebrates his 87th birthday today.

angles.name: John Cleese's Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 87th Birthday to the comedy icon, John Cleese! From silly walks to hotel mayhem, what's your favourite John Cleese moment?

events.title: The Beatles released 'The White Album' in 1968
events.blurb: The Beatles' self-titled double album, commonly known as 'The White Album', was released on November 22, 1968.

angles.name: White Album Anniversary
angles.copy: 58 years ago, The Beatles gave us 'The White Album'. A sprawling, diverse masterpiece. Which track is your hidden gem from this iconic double LP?
